mechanism_narrative: Strong growth in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects robust consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings and supports higher equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS growth on 20-day forward returns is not significantly positive (rolling 3-year average IC < 0.02) or the signal fails to predict direction in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate beyond the natural rate signals economic distress, prompting earnings downgrades and risk aversion, which results in negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns after UNRATE spikes are not significantly negative (mean return ≤ 0) or the hit rate is below 40%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates combined with rising housing starts indicate an accommodative monetary policy stimulating construction activity, which bolsters economic growth and equity prices through multiplier effects.
failure_condition: The interaction term (FEDFUNDS * HOUST) has no significant predictive power for 60-day forward returns in a regression (p-value > 0.10) or the direction of the signal is consistently wrong.

mechanism_narrative: During economic expansions, rising industrial production indicates strong aggregate demand and supports equity returns; during recessions, rising industrial production may be fleeting inventory effects and does not signal recovery.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth is not significantly positively correlated with 20-day forward returns during expansion periods (Spearman rank correlation < 0.1) or if the signal is reversed in expansions, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation erodes real earnings and forces tighter monetary policy, which depresses equity valuations over short horizons as discount rates increase.
failure_condition: If CPI changes do not show a significant negative correlation with 5-day forward returns (rolling 1-year t-stat < -1.96) or the direction is positive in a majority of subperiods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in housing starts below a key threshold signals a housing sector downturn, which is a leading indicator of broader economic weakness and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 10% year-over-year
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns after a HOUST YoY decline of >10% are not negative (mean return >= 0) or the signal shows no predictive accuracy over the evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in payroll employment and industrial production indicate a synchronized expansion in labor and manufacturing, reinforcing each other and generating broad-based economic momentum that lifts equity markets.
failure_condition: If the multiplicative interaction between PAYEMS and INDPRO growth does not significantly improve predictive performance over univariate models (out-of-sample R² increase < 0.01) or the signal direction is inconsistent.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts are a particularly powerful predictor of equity returns when the federal funds rate is very low, as the housing channel of monetary transmission is most potent.
failure_condition: If the predictive power of HOUST for 60-day returns is not significantly higher in low-FEDFUNDS regimes (regime dummy interaction p-value > 0.10) or if the coefficient sign is opposite in that regime.
